# Troubleshooting Playbook

## Current Sample Size

- Candidate replies: 37
- Strong examples: 16

## Common Tags

- WF Support
- WF Sales
- SLA Breach
- QuadPro
- Tech Support (FC)

## Example Reply Patterns

> Hi Brad,
> 
> Thank you for providing me with this information.
> 
> Please find the map of the Verizon towers in your area; your location will be circled.
> 
> You can view this map here.
> 
> Looks like you have 2 high-frequency towers in your area. The tower to your south is the one serving your area currently; however, both would be viable if using a directional antenna. Please keep these directions in mind when installing an antenna system.
> 
> In your case, I would recommend the QuadPro, as it is compatible with your gateway.
> 
> As for what side of the building would be best, you can test both the north and south sides and install the antenna on the side that delivers the best performance. 
> 
> Regarding latency, latency is primarily controlled by distance from the tower and the gateway hardware; unfortunately, an antenna can't influence this.
> 
> Please let me know what your thoughts are in terms of cable runs. Keep in mind the cabling will be in increments of 10-ft and 20-ft. As soon as I have that information, I can create a quote for you.
> 
> Let me know your thoughts on the recommendation.
> 
> Best,

> Hi Brad,
> 
> Thank you so much for reaching out,
> 
> I'm Ruan from the Sales and Support team at Waveform, and I'd be more than happy to assist you in any way I can.
> 
> I want to make sure that we recommend the best solution for your needs. Before we proceed with a recommendation, there are a few general questions that will help us move on to the next steps that I’ve listed below:
> 
> What is the address where the setup will be done? (For reviewing cell tower maps.)
> 
> What are your current data speeds, with and without the antenna?
> 
> How would you describe your internet usage? (e.g., streaming, working from home, gaming, etc.)
> 
> Regarding the 75-ft cable, I would definitely not recommend having such a long cable run. Signal degrades over distance in the cable. I would recommend not exceeding 30 ft of cable from the antenna to the gateway. The loss would be of such a nature that it might render the antenna ineffective at 75-ft.
> 
> If you have any questions or concerns, please let me know!
> 
> Hope you have a lovely day.
> 
> Best,

> Hi Wyatt, 
> 
> Thanks so much for reaching out!
> 
> I'll include a document for you to use to test the signal on the G4SE which should help me lock down the issue you are facing. 
> 
> Outside of this, could you provide me with the following info? 
> 
> What is the address where the setup will be done? (For double checking the cell tower maps.)
> 
> What are your current data speeds, with and without the antenna?
> 
> How would you describe your internet usage? (e.g., streaming, working from home, gaming, etc.)
> 
> As for locating the tower you are connecting to, it won't be quite as simple as using the tower identifier on the app, as T-Mobile encrypts the cell ID. It's possible to convert it and check the tower, but honestly it's not worth really knowing, as the home internet setups will frequently switch to different towers based on the low priority of the setup and congestion. So what I normally suggest is optimizing the antenna based on metrics and positioning. 
> 
> Please let me know if you have any questions or need assistance with the app.
